summary: Full backend (pytest, 23/23 passing) + frontend (Playwright) coverage on Railway Cleanliness Inspector. Identified and fixed one CRITICAL bug: ai_service was imported BEFORE load_dotenv in server.py, so EMERGENT_LLM_KEY was None and every photo fell back to the 'AI analysis error' stub. After re-ordering imports, Claude Sonnet 4.5 vision now returns real ratings, area_breakdown, issues, and recommendations (verified end-to-end via UI: Mumbai CST photo scored 35 / Unclean with detailed analysis). All other flows confirmed working: admin/SM login, KPI cards, inspections list+filters, inspection detail with photo Dialog (image served via /api/files with ?auth= token, naturalWidth=640), AI verdict override, reports page, user/station/share-link CRUD, SM upload, public share upload (Ahmedabad Jn / Thank you), invalid+revoked tokens rejected, SM authorization (cannot list users/stations/share-links/reports).

minor.issue: Playwright did not see a 'download' event after clicking export-csv-btn within 10s. Likely client-side blob/href download path that bypasses headless download interception. UI button exists and is wired; main agent should verify CSV is actually triggered (e.g., via <a download> blob URL) in real browser.

issues: Recharts emits repeated 'width(-1) height(-1)' warnings due to ResponsiveContainer with no explicit minWidth/minHeight. Non-blocking but should be cleaned up.

action_items: Verify CSV export download in headed browser (button works, but headless 'download' event not fired in 10s).
Optional: silence Recharts ResponsiveContainer warnings by setting minWidth/minHeight on chart containers.
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED by testing agent: ai_service.py reads EMERGENT_LLM_KEY at import time (module load). In server.py, `from ai_service import analyze_image` was happening BEFORE load_dotenv(ROOT_DIR/.env). As a result, EMERGENT_LLM_KEY was None at runtime and every upload fell through to the fallback stub ('AI analysis error: EMERGENT_LLM_KEY not configured'). I moved load_dotenv ABOVE the local imports (auth, ai_service, storage) in /app/backend/server.py. Recommend instead reading EMERGENT_LLM_KEY lazily inside analyze_image() to be robust to import order in future.
Class-scoped pytest fixture defined as instance method (deprecation warning). Cosmetic – tests still pass.
list_inspections SM-strip path: if photos[0] doesn't exist the `or True` branch trivially passes — handled, but the strip silently drops `ai_analysis`; consider returning explicit fields documented.
Reports `date_to` uses string concatenation `+ 'T23:59:59'`. Works because created_at is stored as ISO with timezone but could mismatch if timezone offset present. Acceptable for now.
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (moved load_dotenv above local imports, added noqa: E402)

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all major flows green; CSV download intercept inconclusive in headless)

seed_data_creation: Created in backend tests: TEST_ stations/users (cleaned up), one share link, multiple inspections with real Claude AI analysis (4 inspections + 1 public + override applied).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend at /app/backend/tests/test_railway_cleanliness.py covers auth, stations, users, uploads (with real PIL JPEGs), AI analysis, override, public share, reports, authorization. Run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. Note: AI integration is real (Claude Sonnet 4.5 via emergentintegrations). Each upload-and-persist test takes ~5-10s due to vision call.
rca of the issue: Module-load-time env read: EMERGENT_LLM_KEY captured to a module-level constant in ai_service.py at import; server.py imported ai_service before invoking load_dotenv, so the key was None. Reproduction: upload any photo via /api/inspections/upload — response had ai_analysis.issues=['AI analysis error: EMERGENT_LLM_KEY not configured'] and aggregate_rating='Unclean'/score=50. Mitigation applied: re-ordered load_dotenv before local imports. Verified by re-running upload tests and observing 'LiteLLM completion() model=claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929' in backend logs and real AI output (Mumbai CST score=35 with proper issues/recommendations) in the UI.
